title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA implements BCH ML bias-adjusted three-step estimators latent class analysis (LCA) covariates distal outcomes, following methodological framework BCH Vermunt’s ML approaches Bakk, Tekle & Vermunt (2013). tseLCA also builds top two-step LCA estimation procedure outlined Bakk & Kuha (2018), using R package multilevLCA efficient measurement model estimation Lyrvall et al. (2025). tseLCA provides analytic sandwich variance estimation propagates measurement uncertainty classification-error correction final step. three-step approach separates model : Step 1 — Estimate LCA measurement model (class-conditional item probabilities class prevalences). Step 2 — Assign posterior class probabilities compute misclassification matrix. Step 3 — Estimate structural model (covariate effects distal outcome means) using bias-adjusted weights.

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: built-data-generating process replicates design Bakk & Kuha (2018). dataset six binary indicators (Y1,…,Y6Y_1, \ldots, Y_6) drawn three-class LCA, plus either covariate Zp∼Uniform{1,…,5}Z_p \sim \text{Uniform}\{1,\ldots,5\} predicting class membership, continuous distal outcome ZoZ_o predicted class membership.
code: # High separation: P(Y_h = 1 | class) = 0.9 / 0.1 d <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "high",   scenario = "covariate",   seed = 1 ) head(d) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6 X Zp #> 1  1  1  1  0  0  0 2  1 #> 2  0  0  0  0  0  0 3  4 #> 3  1  1  1  1  1  1 1  1 #> 4  1  1  1  1  1  0 2  2 #> 5  0  0  0  0  0  0 3  5 #> 6  1  1  1  1  1  1 1  3 # Low separation: P(Y_h = 1 | class) = 0.7 / 0.3 # Zp and X are identical to 'd' because seed = 1 d.low <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "low",   scenario = "covariate",   seed = 1 ) head(d.low) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6 X Zp #> 1  1  0  0  0  0  0 2  1 #> 2  0  0  0  1  1  0 3  4 #> 3  1  1  1  1  1  1 1  1 #> 4  1  1  1  1  1  1 2  2 #> 5  0  0  0  0  0  0 3  5 #> 6  1  1  1  0  1  1 1  3

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: three_step() Zp.names Zo.name fits measurement model , returning tseLCA_measurement object. Internally calls multilevLCA::multiLCA() random restarts entropy R2R^2 low. low separation measurement model can struggle find global maximum. Use iter.measurement trigger number random restarts whenever entropy R2R^2 falls R2.threshold. plot() S3 method delegates multilevLCA’s item-profile plot.
code: d.measurement <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   measurement.tol = 1e-8 ) summary(d.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -1467.9716 #> AIC            : 2975.9433 #> BIC            : 3060.2354 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8745 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.3468 #> P(C2) 0.2950 #> P(C3) 0.3582 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.8920 0.8682 0.1187 #> P(Y2|C) 0.9340 0.8954 0.1283 #> P(Y3|C) 0.8893 0.8915 0.1077 #> P(Y4|C) 0.8473 0.0680 0.1007 #> P(Y5|C) 0.9131 0.0773 0.0908 #> P(Y6|C) 0.9018 0.0882 0.1040 d.low.measurement <- three_step(   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   iter.measurement = 10,   R2.threshold = 0.9 ) summary(d.low.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -2006.5242 #> AIC            : 4053.0483 #> BIC            : 4137.3405 #> Entropy R²     : 0.4447 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.4551 #> P(C2) 0.1723 #> P(C3) 0.3726 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.6411 0.7435 0.3010 #> P(Y2|C) 0.8222 0.7303 0.2255 #> P(Y3|C) 0.6894 0.4495 0.3511 #> P(Y4|C) 0.6122 0.2426 0.2674 #> P(Y5|C) 0.6651 0.0661 0.3165 #> P(Y6|C) 0.6540 0.1105 0.3632 plot(d.measurement)

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: fitZ_from_fit0() fixes measurement parameters Step-1 values estimates multinomial logit coefficients γ\gamma via EM. two-step estimates serve starting values Step 3 generally close final three-step estimates. Starting values high-separation fit can passed low-separation fit help converge.
code: d.fitZ <- fitZ_from_fit0(   fit0 = d.measurement$measurement_model$fit0,   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   Zp.names = "Zp" ) # True slopes: -1 (C2) and +1 (C3) relative to C1 d.fitZ$mGamma #>                   C2        C3 #> Intercept  1.7790263 -4.170998 #> Zp        -0.8401332  1.148593 d.low.fitZ <- fitZ_from_fit0(   fit0 = d.low.measurement$measurement_model$fit0,   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   Zp.names = "Zp",   starting_val = d.fitZ$mGamma ) d.low.fitZ$mGamma #>                   C2        C3 #> Intercept  0.1647684 -3.396233 #> Zp        -0.5668117  0.937924

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: single three_step() call handles three steps. default uses ML correction Vermunt (2010) modal class assignment. standard coef() vcov() S3 methods work tseLCA object.

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: modal assignment (use.modal.assignment = TRUE, default), Jacobian measurement-uncertainty correction mathematically defined. Setting use.modal.assignment = FALSE uses soft posterior weights throughout, giving analytic Jacobian recommended separation moderate low. use.modal.assignment = TRUE, Jacobian ∂θ2∂θ1\frac{\partial\theta_2}{\partial\theta_1} computed using full posterior weights (e.g., behaving use.modal.assignment = FALSE) maintain well-defined derivatives, though three-step estimates still computed modal assignment specified. different negligible separation high.
code: d.three_step.prop <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.modal.assignment = FALSE ) summary(d.three_step.prop) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -1338.3943 #> AIC            : 2756.7886 #> BIC            : 2925.3730 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.7790 -4.1710 #> Zp        -0.8401  1.1486 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.8671    0.3655  5.1087 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8756    0.1581 -5.5374 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -4.5000    0.8015 -5.6142 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.2258    0.2006  6.1098 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: Setting use.simple.cov = TRUE skips measurement-uncertainty correction returns robust sandwich SEs Step 3 . separation high correction negligible, useful computational shortcut large samples.
code: d.three_step.simple <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.simple.cov = TRUE ) summary(d.three_step.simple) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -1338.2884 #> AIC            : 2756.5768 #> BIC            : 2925.1611 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.7790 -4.1710 #> Zp        -0.8401  1.1486 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.8739    0.3145  5.9578 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8833    0.1323 -6.6760 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -4.3703    0.6863 -6.3681 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.1945    0.1722  6.9378 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: BCH correction Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars (2004) available via use.bch = TRUE. works well high separation can produce ill-conditioned Hessian separation low (resulting covariance matrix positive semi-definite), case ML estimator preferred. BCH low-separation data can fail produce positive semi-definite Hessian. ML estimator proportional assignment reliable setting.
code: d.three_step.bch <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.bch = TRUE ) summary(d.three_step.bch) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : BCH #> Log-likelihood : -1338.4071 #> AIC            : 2756.8142 #> BIC            : 2925.3985 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.7790 -4.1710 #> Zp        -0.8401  1.1486 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.9274    0.3346  5.7596 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.9084    0.1468 -6.1880 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -3.9789    0.6458 -6.1609 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.1066    0.1636  6.7662 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 # Not run in vignette build (slow and and produces warnings) bch.fail <- three_step(   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.bch = TRUE,   maxIter.measurement = 2000,   iter.measurement = 10 ) # Preferred approach for low separation d.low.three_step.prop <- three_step(   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.modal.assignment = FALSE ) summary(d.low.three_step.prop) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -1974.5489 #> AIC            : 4029.0977 #> BIC            : 4197.6821 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  0.1594 -3.3877 #> Zp        -0.5637  0.9358 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   0.9140    2.5972  0.3519 0.7249      #> Zp:C2         -1.0336    2.8770 -0.3593 0.7194      #> Intercept:C3  -4.2276    2.7433 -1.5411 0.1233      #> Zp:C3          1.1703    0.6606  1.7717 0.0764  .   #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: default, class 1 ("C1") reference category multinomial logit parameterisation. rebase argument changes . Estimates reparameterised consistently: log-likelihoods invariant, coefficients satisfy transitivity relation log⁡(πt/πj)=log⁡(πt/π1)−log⁡(πj/π1)\log(\pi_t / \pi_j) = \log(\pi_t / \pi_1) - \log(\pi_j / \pi_1).

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA uses two-pass row-filtering strategy matches multilevLCA’s approach measurement model allowing observations Steps 1 2 Step 3. incomplete = FALSE (default), row missing indicator dropped measurement model estimated. incomplete = TRUE, fully-missing rows dropped; partially observed rows contribute measurement model via FIML. Regardless incomplete, Step 3 drops row missing covariate. rows used Step 3 subset used Steps 1 2. FIML measurement model can passed reused covariate step sparse data.
code: set.seed(42) d.new <- generate_data(500, separation = "high", seed = 3) sparsity <- 0.1 missing <- 1 -   matrix(     rbinom(prod(dim(d.new)), size = 1, prob = sparsity),     nrow = nrow(d.new),     ncol = ncol(d.new)   ) missing[missing == 0] <- NA_real_ d.sparse <- d.new * missing head(d.sparse) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6  X Zp #> 1  0  0 NA  0  0  0  3  5 #> 2  1  1 NA  1  1  1  1  2 #> 3  0  0  1  0  0  1  3  4 #> 4  1  1  1  1  1  1  1  4 #> 5  0  1  1  1  1  1 NA  2 #> 6  1  0 NA  0  0  0  3  3 d.sparse.measurement <- three_step(   data = d.sparse,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   incomplete = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> 242 row(s) dropped from measurement/classification steps (missing Y). # Rows dropped = number of rows with at least one missing Y sum(apply(d.sparse[, paste0("Y", 1:6)], 1, \(x) any(is.na(x)))) #> [1] 242 summary(d.sparse.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -763.0984 #> AIC            : 1566.1968 #> BIC            : 1637.2559 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8835 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.2914 #> P(C2) 0.3924 #> P(C3) 0.3162 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.9038 0.9015 0.1078 #> P(Y2|C) 0.8920 0.9116 0.0938 #> P(Y3|C) 0.8558 0.8980 0.1073 #> P(Y4|C) 0.9546 0.0841 0.0945 #> P(Y5|C) 0.8999 0.1121 0.1224 #> P(Y6|C) 0.9116 0.1262 0.1677 d.sparse.measurement2 <- three_step(   data = d.sparse,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   incomplete = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE ) summary(d.sparse.measurement2) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -1372.3013 #> AIC            : 2784.6025 #> BIC            : 2868.8947 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8376 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.3064 #> P(C2) 0.3764 #> P(C3) 0.3171 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.8697 0.8820 0.0864 #> P(Y2|C) 0.8965 0.8784 0.1405 #> P(Y3|C) 0.8527 0.8662 0.0796 #> P(Y4|C) 0.9424 0.0817 0.0902 #> P(Y5|C) 0.9159 0.1019 0.1156 #> P(Y6|C) 0.9106 0.1184 0.1592 d.sparse.three_step <- three_step(   data = d.sparse,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   incomplete = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> 43 row(s) excluded from covariate step (missing Z). #> fitZ EM converged in 9 iterations. #> 43 row(s) excluded from covariate step (missing Z). #> EM converged in 6 iterations. # title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA supports polytomous indicators, following multilevLCA’s convention item categories coded consecutive integers starting 0. reproduce example poLCA package.
code: data(election, package = "poLCA") elec <- election elec.items <- colnames(election)[1:12]  # Recode to 0-based integers as required by multilevLCA elec[, elec.items] <- lapply(elec[, elec.items], \(x) as.integer(x) - 1L) elec.measurement <- three_step(   data = elec,   Y.names = elec.items,   n_classes = 3,   #The poLCA example drops any row with a missing cell   incomplete = FALSE )  elec.three_step <- three_step(   data = elec,   Y.names = elec.items,   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = c("PARTY"),   step1 = elec.measurement$measurement_model,   incomplete = FALSE,   #With the neutral group as the base-category   rebase = "C3" ) summary(elec.three_step) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -16278.0242 #> AIC            : 32852.0485 #> BIC            : 33617.2262 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C1      C2 #> Intercept -2.5781  1.8687 #> PARTY      0.4289 -0.6983 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C1  -2.4701    2.6106 -0.9462 0.3441      #> PARTY:C1       0.4077    0.5719  0.7130 0.4758      #> Intercept:C2   1.7324    1.1090  1.5622 0.1183      #> PARTY:C2      -0.6727    0.3774 -1.7824 0.0747  .   #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 party.x <- seq(from = 1, to = 7, length.out = 101) pidmat <- cbind(1, party.x) exb <- exp(pidmat %*% coef(elec.three_step))  matplot(   party.x,   (cbind(1, exb)) / (1 + rowSums(exb)),   ylim = c(0, 1),   type = "l",   lwd = 3,   col = 1,   xlab = "Party ID: strong Democratic (1) to strong Republican (7)",   ylab = "Probability of latent class membership",   main = "Party ID as a predictor of candidate affinity class", ) text(3.9, 0.60, "Other") text(6.2, 0.6, "Bush affinity") text(2.0, 0.65, "Gore affinity")

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: distal outcomes (Zo←X→YZ_o \leftarrow X \rightarrow Y), supply Zo.name family argument. available families "gaussian" (default), "poisson", "binomial". ML BCH estimators available.
code: d.distal <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "high",   scenario = "distal",   seed = 4 ) # True class means: mu = (0, 1, -1) for C1, C2, C3 d.distal.measurement <- three_step(   data = d.distal,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3 )  # ML estimator d.distal.three_step.ml <- three_step(   data = d.distal,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zo.name = "Zo",   step1 = d.distal.measurement$measurement_model,   use.modal.assignment = FALSE,   family = "gaussian" )  # BCH estimator: closed-form M-step for distal outcomes d.distal.three_step.bch <- three_step(   data = d.distal,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zo.name = "Zo",   step1 = d.distal.measurement$measurement_model,   use.modal.assignment = FALSE,   use.bch = TRUE,   family = "gaussian" )  summary(d.distal.three_step.ml) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Distal Outcome Model ------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Family         : gaussian #>  #> Distal outcome estimates by class: #>              Estimate Std.Error  z.value     p.value #> mu_C1 (mean)  -1.0899    0.0813 -13.4095 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C2 (mean)   1.0012    0.0830  12.0690 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C3 (mean)   0.0450    0.0866   0.5194 0.6035      #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 summary(d.distal.three_step.bch) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Distal Outcome Model ------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : BCH #> Family         : gaussian #>  #> Distal outcome estimates by class: #>              Estimate Std.Error  z.value     p.value #> mu_C1 (mean)  -1.0906    0.0862 -12.6567 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C2 (mean)   0.9291    0.0805  11.5410 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C3 (mean)   0.0592    0.0837   0.7066 0.4798      #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA (Three-Step Estimation Latent Class Analysis) introduces bias-adjusted three-step estimation structural latent class models R. package provides comprehensive framework estimating latent class models covariates distal outcomes preserving measurement structure established class formation. Building upon efficient measurement-model estimation procedures implemented multilevLCA, tseLCA extends existing functionality modern three-step estimators, classification-error corrections, variance estimation procedures appropriately account uncertainty latent class measurement stage.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA first R package provide unified implementation modern bias-adjusted three-step estimators latent class analysis. contrast traditional one-step approaches (implemented popular package, poLCA), inclusion covariates may alter underlying latent class definitions, three-step methods preserve measurement model estimated first stage subsequently adjust classification error estimating structural relationships. package implements BCH- ML-based three-step estimators sandwich variance estimators propagate uncertainty measurement model classification-error correction process.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Unlike conventional latent class software uses one-step estimation approach, tseLCA allows measurement structural models estimated using different datasets. flexibility enables researchers calibrate measurement model primary reference sample subsequently apply resulting class definitions external dataset.

what: Support for Multiple Distal Outcome Types
title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA provides native support broad range distal outcome distributions, including: Continuous outcomes (Gaussian) Count outcomes (Poisson) Binary outcomes (Bernoulli).

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Latent class estimation often susceptible local maxima convergence suboptimal solutions. improve estimation reliability, tseLCA incorporates automated diagnostic procedures monitor model quality measurement-model estimation.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Following similar approach multilevLCA, tseLCA employs Full-Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML) estimation accommodate partially observed response patterns without discarding incomplete observations.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: can install development version tseLCA GitHub like : read introductory vignette package’s webpage : https://SamLeeBYU.github.io/tseLCA/articles/tseLCA-workflow.html
code: # Install pak if it is not already available if (!require("pak")) {   install.packages("pak") }  # Install tseLCA from the GitHub repository pak::pak("SamLeeBYU/tseLCA")

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: basic example shows simulate data run three-step LCA covariate single function call:
code: library(tseLCA)  # 1. Generate synthetic data  # (3 classes, 6 dichotomous items, and a multinomial logit covariate 'Zp') d <- generate_data(   n = 500,    separation = "high",    scenario = "covariate",    seed = 1 )  # 2. Estimate the three-step model # This automatically fits the measurement model and estimates covariate effects fit <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   # Proportional assignment is recommended for better uncertainty propagation   use.modal.assignment = FALSE )  # 3. View the measurement and structural model estimates summary(fit)

title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
text: data data.frame. Y.names Character vector indicator column names. n_classes Integer. Number latent classes. Zp.names Character vector covariate column names, NULL. Zo.name Single character name distal outcome column, NULL. step1 Pre-fitted Step-1 object (output lca_step1()), NULL. use.two.step Logical. Use two-step starting values Step 3. use.modal.assignment Logical. Use modal (hard) class assignment. include.intercept Logical. Include intercept covariate model. use.simple.cov Logical. Skip measurement-uncertainty correction. incomplete Logical. FIML missing indicators. boundary.tol Boundary tolerance phi parameters. maxIter.measurement Maximum EM iterations Step 1. measurement.tol Convergence tolerance Step 1. covariate.tol Convergence tolerance Step 3. iter.measurement Random restarts entropy R\(^2\) low. R2.threshold Entropy R\(^2\) restart threshold. use.bch Logical. Use BCH weights instead ML. em.maxIter Maximum EM iterations Step 3. get.twostep.vcov Logical. TRUE, call fitZ_from_multiLCA() obtain multilevLCA's corrected standard errors two-step gamma estimates store $two_step_vcov. Requires multilevLCA. Default FALSE. rebase Character (e.g. "C1", "C2") integer specifying latent class use reference category multinomial logit Steps 2 3. measurement model permuted class becomes column 1 structural estimation. Default "C1". family One "gaussian" (default), "poisson", "binomial". correct.spec Logical. Use model-robust (outer-product) Hessian. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
code: d <- generate_data(n = 200, separation = "high",                    scenario = "covariate", seed = 1)  # Measurement model only fit_m <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3) summary(fit_m) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -599.3319 #> AIC            : 1238.6639 #> BIC            : 1304.6302 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8635 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.3202 #> P(C2) 0.3664 #> P(C3) 0.3134 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.8672 0.8462 0.0871 #> P(Y2|C) 0.8663 0.9126 0.1540 #> P(Y3|C) 0.9669 0.9570 0.1746 #> P(Y4|C) 0.9591 0.0955 0.1368 #> P(Y5|C) 0.8865 0.1284 0.1725 #> P(Y6|C) 0.8980 0.1358 0.1043   # ML three-step with simple SEs (fast) fit <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -542.3379 #> AIC            : 1164.6758 #> BIC            : 1296.6084 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.8853 -5.3376 #> Zp        -0.7516  1.4098 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   2.0295    0.4900  4.1416 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8192    0.1891 -4.3323 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -5.4875    1.2680 -4.3278 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.4540    0.3215  4.5223 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 coef(fit) #>                   C2        C3 #> Intercept  2.0294548 -5.487549 #> Zp        -0.8191555  1.453963 vcov(fit) #>              Intercept:C2         Zp:C2  Intercept:C3        Zp:C3 #> Intercept:C2  0.240113667 -0.0840137884  0.0197404469 -0.009578627 #> Zp:C2        -0.084013788  0.0357512662 -0.0001681731  0.003017456 #> Intercept:C3  0.019740447 -0.0001681731  1.6077453606 -0.400263916 #> Zp:C3        -0.009578627  0.0030174563 -0.4002639161  0.103368072  # Full measurement-uncertainty correction fit_cor <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                       Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = FALSE,                       use.modal.assignment = FALSE) summary(fit_cor) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -542.2847 #> AIC            : 1164.5695 #> BIC            : 1296.5022 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.8853 -5.3376 #> Zp        -0.7516  1.4098 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.9436    0.6833  2.8446 0.0044  **  #> Zp:C2         -0.7810    0.2805 -2.7840 0.0054  **  #> Intercept:C3  -5.3427    2.4344 -2.1947 0.0282  *   #> Zp:C3          1.4236    0.6050  2.3530 0.0186  *   #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # BCH estimator fit_bch <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                       Zp.names = "Zp", use.bch = TRUE,                       use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit_bch) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : BCH #> Log-likelihood : -542.5750 #> AIC            : 1165.1501 #> BIC            : 1297.0828 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.8853 -5.3376 #> Zp        -0.7516  1.4098 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.9506    0.5093  3.8296 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8085    0.2107 -3.8371 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -6.0386    1.8318 -3.2965 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.5939    0.4508  3.5359 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Change reference class fit_c2 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                      Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = TRUE,                      rebase = "C2") summary(fit_c2) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -542.3379 #> AIC            : 1164.6758 #> BIC            : 1296.6084 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C1      C3 #> Intercept -1.8853 -7.2228 #> Zp         0.7516  2.1614 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C1  -2.0295    0.4900 -4.1416 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C1          0.8192    0.1891  4.3323 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -7.5170    1.3448 -5.5899 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          2.2731    0.3648  6.2310 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Gaussian distal outcome d2 <- generate_data(200, "high", "distal", seed = 2) fit_dis <- three_step(d2, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                       Zo.name = "Zo", family = "gaussian",                       use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit_dis) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Distal Outcome Model ------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Family         : gaussian #>  #> Distal outcome estimates by class: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> mu_C1 (mean)  -0.8837    0.1185 -7.4579 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C2 (mean)   0.9948    0.1188  8.3712 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C3 (mean)   0.1488    0.1510  0.9852 0.3245      #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Pass a pre-fitted measurement model fit_step1 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3) fit2 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                    Zp.names = "Zp", step1 = fit_step1,                    use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit2) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -542.3379 #> AIC            : 1164.6758 #> BIC            : 1296.6084 #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.8853 -5.3376 #> Zp        -0.7516  1.4098 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   2.0295    0.4900  4.1416 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8192    0.1891 -4.3323 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -5.4875    1.2680 -4.3278 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          1.4540    0.3215  4.5223 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Plot item-response profiles plot(fit)

what: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: tseLCA implements bias-adjusted three-step estimators structural latent class models covariates distal outcomes. Building efficient measurement-model estimation procedures multilevLCA, tseLCA extends existing functionality modern three-step estimators, classification-error corrections, analytic sandwich variance estimation propagates uncertainty latent class measurement stage final structural parameter estimates.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Three-step methods separate latent class model three stages, preserving measurement structure established class formation. contrast one-step approaches (e.g. poLCA), including covariates may alter underlying latent class definitions, three-step methods fix measurement model estimating structural relationships adjust classification error final stage. Step 1 Estimate LCA measurement model – class-conditional item-response probabilities class prevalences estimated multilevLCA. Step 2 Assign posterior class probabilities compute T x T misclassification matrix. Step 3 Estimate structural model using bias-adjusted weights (ML BCH correction) sandwich variance estimation.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: three_step Full three-step estimation pipeline. Accepts covariates (Zp.names), distal outcomes (Zo.name), . Handles measurement, classification, structural estimation single call. lca_step1 Step-1 measurement model via multilevLCA. Also computes two-step covariate initialisation via fitZ_from_fit0 Zp.names supplied. fitZ_from_fit0 Two-step covariate estimation fixing measurement parameters Step-1 values estimating multinomial logit coefficients via EM. Returns starting values Step 3. fitZ_from_multiLCA Two-step covariate estimation via multiLCA(fixedpars = 1), returning multilevLCA's bias-corrected standard errors. Called get.twostep.vcov = TRUE three_step. generate_data Simulate data Bakk & Kuha (2018) three-class LCA design covariate distal outcome scenarios across three separation levels. generate_all_conditions Batch simulation across scenarios, separation levels, sample sizes replication studies.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: ML (default) Vermunt (2010) ML correction uses weighted pseudo-likelihood misclassification matrix bias adjustment. Set use.bch = FALSE (default). BCH Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars (2004) correction inverts misclassification matrix obtain direct class weights. Set use.bch = TRUE. Works well high separation may produce ill-conditioned Hessian low separation.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Simple (robust) Sandwich SEs Step 3 (use.simple.cov = TRUE). Efficient separation high measurement uncertainty negligible. Full correction Analytic propagation measurement uncertainty Step 1 classification-error correction (use.simple.cov = FALSE, default). Uses analytic Jacobian computed soft (proportional) posteriors regardless use.modal.assignment setting.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Binary polytomous indicators (following multilevLCA coding conventions: consecutive integers 0). Gaussian, Poisson, binomial distal outcome families. Full-information maximum likelihood (FIML) partially observed indicator patterns (incomplete = TRUE). Flexible measurement structural samples: measurement model can estimated reference sample applied different analysis sample via step1 argument. Arbitrary reference class via rebase argument. S3 methods: print, summary, coef, vcov, plot four return subclasses (tseLCA_measurement, tseLCA_covariate, tseLCA_distal, tseLCA_both).

title: Extract the variance-covariance matrix from a tseLCA model object — vcov.tseLCA_measurement
code: d    <- generate_data(100, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) fit_m <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3) vcov(fit_m)   # returns NULL with a message #> No variance-covariance matrix available for measurement-only models. # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit)   # Q*(T-1) x Q*(T-1) vcov matrix with named rows/cols #>              Intercept:C2         Zp:C2  Intercept:C3        Zp:C3 #> Intercept:C2  0.240113667 -0.0840137884  0.0197404469 -0.009578627 #> Zp:C2        -0.084013788  0.0357512662 -0.0001681731  0.003017456 #> Intercept:C3  0.019740447 -0.0001681731  1.6077453606 -0.400263916 #> Zp:C3        -0.009578627  0.0030174563 -0.4002639161  0.103368072 # } # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "distal", seed = 2) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zo.name = "Zo", use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit)   # T x T vcov matrix with mu_C1..mu_CT row/col names #>               mu_C1         mu_C2         mu_C3 #> mu_C1  0.0140394227  0.0002115275 -0.0001400022 #> mu_C2  0.0002115275  0.0141216292 -0.0006978379 #> mu_C3 -0.0001400022 -0.0006978379  0.0228157389 # } # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) d$Zo <- rnorm(200, mean = c(-1, 0, 1)[d$X], sd = 0.5) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zp.names = "Zp", Zo.name = "Zo",                   use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit, which = "covariate") #>              Intercept:C2         Zp:C2  Intercept:C3        Zp:C3 #> Intercept:C2  0.240113667 -0.0840137884  0.0197404469 -0.009578627 #> Zp:C2        -0.084013788  0.0357512662 -0.0001681731  0.003017456 #> Intercept:C3  0.019740447 -0.0001681731  1.6077453606 -0.400263916 #> Zp:C3        -0.009578627  0.0030174563 -0.4002639161  0.103368072 vcov(fit, which = "distal") #>              mu_C1        mu_C2        mu_C3 #> mu_C1 3.863995e-03 4.056130e-05 1.833309e-05 #> mu_C2 4.056130e-05 4.412576e-03 2.247326e-05 #> mu_C3 1.833309e-05 2.247326e-05 5.646743e-03 # }

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Initial submission CRAN. Implemented BCH ML three-step estimators latent class analysis. Added support Gaussian, Poisson, binomial distal outcome families. Included data-generating process replicating Bakk & Kuha (2018) simulation study. Implemented sandwich variance estimation propagate measurement uncertainty.
